review_scope: Commit dc18279 — FoodMatchingModal, RawInputEditModal, FoodSubRow, Track.tsx

findings.title: Convex subscription churn — every distinct search keystroke creates a new live subscription
findings.description: useQuery creates a live Convex subscription for each distinct set of arguments. With deferredSearchQuery updating on each trim-distinct keystroke, the modal accumulates subscriptions during a search session. useDeferredValue helps batch React renders but does NOT prevent Convex from opening a subscription per distinct query string. Additionally, the default limit of 160 when no search query and no bucket are active is large for a scrollable list in a modal.
findings.fix: Add a debounce hook (300ms) between the raw search query and the value passed to useQuery. This collapses rapid keystrokes into a single subscription. Also consider reducing the default limit from 160 to 50-80.

findings.title: useEffect overrides user's manual canonical selection when server data updates
findings.description: The effect on lines 117-121 runs whenever defaultCandidateCanonical changes. If the user has manually selected a different canonical from the search list, and then the server-side matchCandidates data updates (causing defaultCandidateCanonical to change), this effect silently overwrites the user's explicit selection. This is a UX bug.
findings.fix: Track whether the user has made an explicit selection using a ref (e.g., const userHasSelected = useRef(false)). Set it to true in user-initiated setSelectedCanonical calls, reset it in resetItemState. Guard this effect with if (!userHasSelected.current).

findings.title: Loading spinner always shows on modal open before first Convex response
findings.description: serverSearchOptions is undefined until the first Convex response arrives. The modal always shows a loading spinner in the listbox on open, even when candidate options from the item data are already displayed above.
findings.fix: Only show the loading indicator when the user has actually typed a search query: const isSearchLoading = deferredSearchQuery.length > 0 && serverSearchOptions === undefined;

findings.title: Candidate option buttons lack ARIA selection semantics
findings.description: The candidate option buttons (lines 310-349) are plain <button> elements without role='option' or aria-selected, unlike the listbox options further down (line 458). Screen reader users cannot distinguish selected from unselected candidates.
findings.fix: Add aria-pressed={isSelected} to candidate buttons, or wrap in a listbox container with role='option' on each.

findings.title: Bucket filter buttons lack accessible active state
findings.description: Bucket filter buttons indicate active state only through visual CSS classes. Screen reader users cannot tell which bucket is selected.
findings.fix: Add aria-pressed={isActive} to each bucket button.

findings.title: processLog mutation removed — docstring still claims server reprocessing is triggered
findings.description: The explicit processLog mutation call was removed. The docstring (lines 5-9) still says the pipeline is re-triggered from Stage 1. If processLog was the only reprocessing mechanism, items: [] alone may not trigger it, making the Reprocessing toast misleading.
findings.fix: Verify that updating the log with items: [] triggers server-side reprocessing. Update the docstring to reflect the actual mechanism.

findings.title: Potential stale index access on entry.data.items[matchingItemIndex]
findings.description: If items are reprocessed between when matchingItemIndex was set and when this renders, the index could be out of bounds, yielding undefined passed as item to FoodMatchingModal.
findings.fix: Add a guard: only render FoodMatchingModal when entry.data.items[matchingItemIndex] != null.

findings.title: useFoodLlmMatching hook removed — dead code left behind
findings.description: The hook was removed from Track.tsx but the file src/hooks/useFoodLlmMatching.ts still exists. If LLM matching is now server-side, the hook file is dead code.
findings.fix: Delete src/hooks/useFoodLlmMatching.ts if LLM matching is fully server-side. If still needed, document why it was removed from Track.

findings.title: useEffect dependency array uses Date prototype method references that never change
findings.description: The timer useEffect has [now.getMilliseconds, now.getSeconds] which are always the same function references. The effect runs once on mount, which is correct, but the deps are misleading.
findings.fix: Use an empty dependency array [] with the eslint-disable to honestly reflect intent.

positive_observations: Good use of useDeferredValue for search query batching at the React render level
Proper lazy loading of FoodMatchingModal via React.lazy in both FoodSubRow and Track.tsx
Consistent error handling with getErrorMessage across all async operations
Clean separation of candidate options (server-provided) from full registry search (user-driven)
Proper use of Convex 'skip' to avoid unnecessary subscriptions when modal is closed
Good accessibility baseline with role='listbox', role='option', aria-selected on main search results
Architecturally sound move from client-side static FOOD_REGISTRY filtering to server-side searchFoods query
